info.name: Hiring Backend API
info.description:
# Hiring Backend API

## Quick start
1. Run **Auth → Sign Up** to create your account.
2. Run **Auth → Sign In** — the bearer token is saved to `bearerToken` automatically.
3. Call **Doors → List All Doors** and copy any `id` from the response.
4. Paste that ID into the **doorId** path variable on any Events request (visible in the "Path Variables" tab below the URL).
5. Open the **Console** (View → Show Postman Console) before running encrypted requests — decrypted payloads are printed there.

## How the ECDH encryption works
Every request in a **— ECDH Encrypted** folder has two scripts attached to it:
- **Pre-request Script** — generates a fresh ephemeral P-256 key pair and sets `X-Client-Public-Key`.
- **Tests** — reads `X-Server-Public-Key` from the response header, runs ECDH → HKDF-SHA256 → AES-256-GCM and logs the plaintext to the Console.

Error responses (4xx / 5xx) are **never encrypted** — you can read them directly from the response body.

> **Requires Postman v10.13+** (Web Crypto API must be available in the sandbox).

## Binary log format (Simulation endpoints)
Each simulated event's `logEvent` field is a Base64-encoded binary blob:
```
byte 0-3  → message timestamp, 4 bytes little-endian (zeroed in synthetic data)
byte 4    → logCode: upper nibble = payload byte count, lower nibble = event type id
byte 5..N → payload bytes (little-endian, layout depends on event type)
```
Add `debug=true` to any simulate request to include `parsedEvent` in the response for side-by-side comparison.

variable.description: Set automatically by Sign In — do not edit
Managed by ECDH pre-request scripts — do not edit
Last decrypted payload — updated by ECDH test scripts

item.name: Auth
item.description: User registration and authentication. No bearer token required for these routes.

request.description:
## POST /users/signup

Registers a new user. Returns the created user (password excluded).

### Auth
None required.

### Body
| Field | Type | Rules |
|---|---|---|
| firstName | string | Non-blank |
| lastName | string | Non-blank |
| email | string | Valid e-mail format, must be unique |
| password | string | 4–12 chars, must contain uppercase, number and symbol |

### Response `201 Created`
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "firstName": "Candidate",
  "lastName": "Tester",
  "email": "candidate@example.com"
}
```

### Errors
- `409` — e-mail already registered (`EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS`)
- `422` — validation failed (`fieldErrors` lists the offending fields)

response.name: 422 Validation Failed

exec: pm.test('Sign in must return a token', () => { throw new Error(JSON.stringify(data)); });

request.description:
## POST /users/signin

Authenticates a user and returns a session bearer token. The **Tests** script stores the token in the `bearerToken` collection variable automatically — all subsequent authenticated requests use it.

### Auth
None required.

### Body
| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| email | string | Registered e-mail |
| password | string | Plain-text password |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{ "token": "a1b2c3d4e5f6..." }
```

### Errors
- `401` — wrong e-mail or password (`INVALID_CREDENTIALS`)

item.name: Doors
item.description: Plain (unencrypted) door listing. Requires a valid bearer token.

request.description:
## GET /doors

Returns a paginated list of all doors in the system (5 000 total).

### Auth
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` — required.

### Query parameters
| Param | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| page | 0 | Zero-based page number |
| size | 20 | Items per page |
| sort | — | e.g. `name,asc` or `battery,desc` |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "content": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "serial": "LLKOA1B2C3D4EF",
      "lockMac": "AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF",
      "name": "Museu do Ipiranga",
      "address": "Parque da Independência, São Paulo - SP",
      "latitude": -23.5851,
      "longitude": -46.6089,
      "battery": 87
    }
  ],
  "page": {
    "number": 0,
    "size": 20,
    "totalElements": 5000,
    "totalPages": 250
  }
}
```

### Notes
- Copy any `id` from the response and paste it into the **doorId** path variable on Events requests.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
## GET /doors/find

Returns a paginated list of doors whose name contains the given string (case-insensitive).

### Auth
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` — required.

### Query parameters
| Param | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| name | Yes | Substring to match against door names |
| page | No | Zero-based page number (default 0) |
| size | No | Items per page (default 20) |

### Response `200 OK`
Same paginated shape as `GET /doors`. `totalElements` reflects the filtered count.

### Errors
- `400` — `name` parameter is missing
- `401` — missing or invalid bearer token

response.name: 200 OK — Filtered results

item.name: Doors — ECDH Encrypted
item.description:
Same door listing endpoints, but the server encrypts the response body using ECDH key exchange.

Each request has its full ECDH scripts in the **Pre-request Script** and **Tests** tabs. The decrypted payload is printed to the Postman Console and also stored in `_lastDecryptedResponse`.

**Encrypted response shape:**
```json
{ "iv": "<base64 12-byte IV>", "ciphertext": "<base64 AES-256-GCM ciphertext>" }
```

exec: // ── Generate ephemeral P-256 key pair (fresh per request = forward secrecy) ─
const keyPair    = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveBits']);
console.log('[Crypto] Client public key (SPKI DER / Base64):', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
console.log('[Crypto] No X-Server-Public-Key — response is unencrypted (likely an error).');
// 1. Import server ephemeral public key (SPKI DER)
// 2. Restore client ephemeral private key from collection variable
// 3. ECDH → 256-bit shared secret (x-coordinate of shared EC point)
const rawSecret = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
// 4. HKDF-SHA256 → 32-byte AES key  (info = UTF-8('door-event-api-v1'), salt = 32 zero bytes)
const hkdfKey     = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
{ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') },
// 5. AES-256-GCM decrypt  (IV = 12 bytes, auth tag = last 16 bytes of ciphertext)
const aesKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
pm.test('Response decrypts to valid non-empty JSON', () => { pm.expect(plaintext).to.be.a('string').and.not.be.empty; JSON.parse(plaintext); });

request.description:
## GET /doors — Encrypted

Same as **List All Doors** but with ECDH encryption applied.

### How it works
1. The Pre-request Script generates a P-256 key pair and sends the public key in `X-Client-Public-Key`.
2. The server generates its own ephemeral key pair, derives the shared secret, and encrypts the response.
3. The response body is `{ "iv": "<base64>", "ciphertext": "<base64>" }` instead of plain JSON.
4. The Tests script performs the same ECDH computation on the client side and decrypts the ciphertext.
5. Decrypted content is logged to the Console and stored in `_lastDecryptedResponse`.

### Encrypted response shape
```json
{
  "iv": "<base64-encoded 12-byte IV>",
  "ciphertext": "<base64-encoded AES-256-GCM ciphertext + 16-byte auth tag>"
}
```

### Headers returned by server
| Header | Description |
|---|---|
| X-Server-Public-Key | Server's ephemeral SPKI DER public key (Base64) |

exec: const keyPair    = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveBits']);
console.log('[Crypto] Client public key set:', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
const serverPublicKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', Uint8Array.from(atob(serverPubKeyBase64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('_ephemeralPrivKeyJwk')), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const rawSecret       = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') }, hkdfKey, 256));
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
pm.test('Decrypts to valid JSON', () => { pm.expect(plaintext).to.be.a('string'); JSON.parse(plaintext); });
} catch (err) { console.error('[Crypto] ❌', err.message); pm.test('Decryption must succeed', () => { throw err; }); }

request.description:
## GET /doors/find — Encrypted

Same as **Find Doors by Name** but with ECDH encryption applied to the response.

### Query parameters
| Param | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| name | Yes | Case-insensitive name substring filter |
| page | No | Zero-based page number |
| size | No | Items per page |

See **List All Doors — Encrypted** for the full encryption flow description.

item.name: Events
item.description: Door event history. The door ID is a **path variable** on each request — edit it in the "Path Variables" tab below the URL bar.

variable.description: ID of the door to fetch events for. Get a valid ID from List All Doors.

request.description:
## GET /doors/{doorId}/events

Returns a paginated list of BLE log events recorded for a specific door.

### Auth
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` — required.

### Path variable
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| doorId | Numeric door ID (get one from List All Doors) |

### Query parameters
| Param | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| page | 0 | Zero-based page number |
| size | 20 | Items per page |
| sort | — | e.g. `eventTimestamp,desc` |

### Response `200 OK`
```json
{
  "content": [
    {
      "id": 101,
      "logType": "DOOR_OPEN",
      "eventTimestamp": "2025-06-12T14:32:00",
      "additionalData": []
    },
    {
      "id": 102,
      "logType": "UNLOCK",
      "eventTimestamp": "2025-06-12T14:31:45",
      "additionalData": [
        {"parameterName": "PERMISSION_MODE", "value": "2"},
        {"parameterName": "PERMISSION_ID",   "value": "4815162342"}
      ]
    }
  ],
  "page": { "number": 0, "size": 20, "totalElements": 47, "totalPages": 3 }
}
```

### Errors
- `404` — door not found (`RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND`)
- `401` — missing or invalid bearer token

variable.description: ID of the door to fetch raw events for. Get a valid ID from List All Doors.

query.description: Set to true to include parsedEvent for parser verification

request.description:
## GET /doors/{doorId}/events/raw

Returns the same door events as `GET /doors/{id}/events` but in the **binary wire format** — identical to what `GET /doors/events/simulate` produces.

Each item contains:
- **messageKey** — `{logType}_{eventId}` identifier
- **logEvent** — Base64-encoded raw BLE log entry:
  - bytes 0–3  → timestamp, 4 bytes little-endian (zeroed in seeded data)
  - byte  4    → logCode: upper nibble = payload byte count, lower nibble = event type id
  - bytes 5..N → payload bytes, little-endian, event-type specific
- **parsedEvent** — only when `debug=true`. Contains the expected parser output for verification.

Use this to test your parsing pipeline against **persisted events** (a fixed dataset) rather than freshly generated synthetic data.

### Path variable
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| doorId | Numeric door ID (get one from List All Doors) |

### Query parameters
| Param | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| page | 0 | Zero-based page number |
| size | 20 | Items per page |
| debug | false | Include parsedEvent for comparison |

## GET /doors/999999999/events

Demonstrates the 404 response for a door ID that does not exist in the database.

Error responses from `/doors/**` are **never encrypted** — even when `X-Client-Public-Key` is present — so you can always read them directly.

item.name: Events — ECDH Encrypted
item.description: Same as the Events folder but the response body is encrypted. Edit **doorId** in the path variable on each request.

exec: const keyPair    = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveBits']);
console.log('[Crypto] Client public key set:', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
const serverPublicKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', Uint8Array.from(atob(serverPubKeyBase64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('_ephemeralPrivKeyJwk')), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const rawSecret       = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') }, hkdfKey, 256));
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
pm.test('Decrypts to valid JSON', () => { pm.expect(plaintext).to.be.a('string'); JSON.parse(plaintext); });
} catch (err) { console.error('[Crypto] ❌', err.message); pm.test('Decryption must succeed', () => { throw err; }); }

variable.description: ID of the door to fetch events for. Get a valid ID from List All Doors.

item.name: Events — ECDH Encrypted (Raw)
item.description: Raw binary door events with ECDH encryption applied. Edit **doorId** in the path variable on each request.

console.log('[Crypto] Client public key set:', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
const serverPublicKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', Uint8Array.from(atob(serverPubKeyBase64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('_ephemeralPrivKeyJwk')), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const rawSecret       = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') }, hkdfKey, 256));
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
} catch (err) { console.error('[Crypto] ❌', err.message); pm.test('Decryption must succeed', () => { throw err; }); }

variable.description: ID of the door to fetch raw events for.

query.description: Set to true to include parsedEvent in the decrypted payload

request.description:
## GET /doors/{doorId}/events/raw — Encrypted

Same as **List Raw Door Events** but the server encrypts the response body.

The Tests script decrypts the paginated response and logs each event's `logEvent` (Base64) to the Console. Decode the Base64, parse the binary, and compare against `parsedEvent` (add `debug=true` to enable it).

See **List All Doors — Encrypted** for the full ECDH encryption flow description.

item.name: Simulation
item.description:
## Simulation endpoint — `GET /doors/events/simulate`

Generates synthetic BLE log entries for candidates to implement and verify their parser.

### Response fields
| Field | Always present | Description |
|---|---|---|
| messageKey | Yes | Unique identifier for the generated entry |
| logEvent | Yes | **Base64-encoded raw binary blob** — decode this and parse it |
| parsedEvent | Only when `debug=true` | Expected parser output for side-by-side comparison |

### Binary layout of `logEvent`
```
byte 0-3  → timestamp, 4 bytes little-endian (zeroed in synthetic data)
byte 4    → logCode: upper nibble = payload byte count, lower nibble = event type id
byte 5..N → payload bytes (little-endian, event-type specific)
```

### Available logType values
`SETUP` `DOOR_OPEN` `DOOR_CLOSE` `TOUCH_UNLOCK` `MANUFACTURE` `BATTERY_LOW` `STATUS_PRIVATE` `STATUS_KEY` `STATUS_HALF_OPEN` `STATUS_DFU` `EEPROM_WRITE_ERROR` `EEPROM_CRC_ERROR` `CONFIGURATION` `RESET` `LOG_FULL` `ERROR` `SYSTEM_ERROR` `SCHEDULE_CANCEL` `UNLOCK` `UNLOCK_DENIED` `SCHEDULE_START` `SCHEDULE_FINISH` `SCHEDULE_TOUCH_CANCEL` `SCHEDULE_INIT`

query.description: Set to true to include parsedEvent for comparison

request.description:
## GET /doors/events/simulate — DOOR_OPEN

DOOR_OPEN has **0 payload bytes**. The `logEvent` Base64 decodes to exactly **5 bytes** (4 timestamp + 1 logCode).

The logCode upper nibble will be `0` (no payload). The lower nibble encodes the DOOR_OPEN event type id.

### Expected parsed output (add `debug=true` to see it)
```json
{
  "logType": "DOOR_OPEN",
  "additionalData": []
}
```

## GET /doors/events/simulate — SCHEDULE_INIT

SCHEDULE_INIT has **12 payload bytes** — one of the largest payloads. The `logEvent` decodes to **17 bytes**. LogCode upper nibble = `C` (hex) = 12.

Payload: `USER_ID` (4 bytes `uint32` LE) + `TIME_TO_START` (4 bytes `uint32` LE, Unix timestamp) + `TIME_TO_END` (4 bytes `uint32` LE, Unix timestamp).

### Expected parsed output
```json
{
  "logType": "SCHEDULE_INIT",
  "additionalData": [
    {"parameterName": "USER_ID",       "value": "1001"},
    {"parameterName": "TIME_TO_START", "value": "1718200000"},
    {"parameterName": "TIME_TO_END",   "value": "1718207200"}
  ]
}
```

item.name: All types sampler — change logType freely

query.description: Valid values (case-insensitive): SETUP, DOOR_OPEN, DOOR_CLOSE, TOUCH_UNLOCK, MANUFACTURE, BATTERY_LOW, STATUS_PRIVATE, STATUS_KEY, STATUS_HALF_OPEN, STATUS_DFU, EEPROM_WRITE_ERROR, EEPROM_CRC_ERROR, CONFIGURATION, RESET, LOG_FULL, ERROR, SYSTEM_ERROR, SCHEDULE_CANCEL, UNLOCK, UNLOCK_DENIED, SCHEDULE_START, SCHEDULE_FINISH, SCHEDULE_TOUCH_CANCEL, SCHEDULE_INIT
Set to true to include parsedEvent

request.description:
## GET /doors/events/simulate — Generic sampler

Change `logType` to any of the 24 allowed values to explore different binary payload sizes and structures. Add `debug=true` to reveal `parsedEvent` and verify your parser output.

### All valid logType values
| logType | Payload bytes | Key fields |
|---|---|---|
| SETUP | 0 | — |
| DOOR_OPEN | 0 | — |
| DOOR_CLOSE | 0 | — |
| TOUCH_UNLOCK | 0 | — |
| MANUFACTURE | 0 | — |
| BATTERY_LOW | 1 | BATTERY_LEVEL |
| STATUS_PRIVATE | 1 | STATUS |
| STATUS_KEY | 1 | KEY_STATUS |
| STATUS_HALF_OPEN | 1 | DOOR_STATUS |
| STATUS_DFU | 1 | DFU_STATUS |
| EEPROM_WRITE_ERROR | 2 | EEPROM_ADDRESS |
| EEPROM_CRC_ERROR | 2 | EEPROM_ADDRESS |
| CONFIGURATION | 4 | CONFIGURATION_DATA |
| RESET | 4 | RESET_REASON |
| LOG_FULL | 4 | LOG_COUNT |
| ERROR | 4 | ERROR_CODE |
| SYSTEM_ERROR | 4 | SYSTEM_ERROR_CODE |
| SCHEDULE_CANCEL | 4 | USER_ID |
| UNLOCK | 5 | PERMISSION_MODE + PERMISSION_ID |
| UNLOCK_DENIED | 5 | PERMISSION_MODE + PERMISSION_ID |
| SCHEDULE_START | 5 | USER_ID + SCHEDULE_ID |
| SCHEDULE_FINISH | 5 | USER_ID + SCHEDULE_ID |
| SCHEDULE_TOUCH_CANCEL | 5 | USER_ID + SCHEDULE_ID |
| SCHEDULE_INIT | 12 | USER_ID + TIME_TO_START + TIME_TO_END |

## Error — unknown / disallowed logType

Returns `400 BAD_REQUEST` for any logType that is not in the allowed list. `GENERIC_CMD` is a proprietary type excluded from the simulation API.

The error `description` includes the rejected value to aid debugging.

## Error — count out of range

Returns `400 BAD_REQUEST` when `count` is outside the allowed range of 1–100.

item.name: Simulation — ECDH Encrypted
item.description: Same simulation endpoint with ECDH encryption applied. The Tests script decrypts the response and logs each event's `logEvent` (Base64) to the Console. Add `debug=true` to the query to also log `parsedEvent` for comparison.

exec: const keyPair    = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveBits']);
console.log('[Crypto] Client public key set:', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
const serverPublicKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', Uint8Array.from(atob(serverPubKeyBase64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('_ephemeralPrivKeyJwk')), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const rawSecret       = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') }, hkdfKey, 256));
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] ✅ Decrypted — decode logEvent from Base64 and run through your parser:');
} catch (err) { console.error('[Crypto] ❌', err.message); pm.test('Decryption must succeed', () => { throw err; }); }

query.description: Set to true to include parsedEvent in the decrypted payload

request.description:
## GET /doors/events/simulate?logType=UNLOCK — Encrypted

Generates 5 UNLOCK events and returns them encrypted.

The Tests script decrypts the ciphertext and logs each item's `logEvent` (Base64) to the Console. Decode the Base64, parse the binary (5 payload bytes: PERMISSION_MODE `uint8` + PERMISSION_ID `uint32` LE), and compare your result against `parsedEvent` (visible when `debug=true`).

Add `debug=true` to the query string to include `parsedEvent` in the plaintext so you can check your parsing.

exec: const keyPair    = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveBits']);
console.log('[Crypto] Client public key set:', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
const serverPublicKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', Uint8Array.from(atob(serverPubKeyBase64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('_ephemeralPrivKeyJwk')), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const rawSecret       = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') }, hkdfKey, 256));
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] ✅ Decrypted — decode logEvent from Base64 and run through your parser:');
} catch (err) { console.error('[Crypto] ❌', err.message); pm.test('Decryption must succeed', () => { throw err; }); }

request.description:
## GET /doors/events/simulate?logType=SCHEDULE_INIT — Encrypted

Generates 3 SCHEDULE_INIT events (12 payload bytes each = 17 bytes total per logEvent) and returns them encrypted.

Binary payload: `USER_ID` (4 bytes `uint32` LE) + `TIME_TO_START` (4 bytes `uint32` LE) + `TIME_TO_END` (4 bytes `uint32` LE).

This is one of the most complex payload types — good for testing multi-field little-endian parsing. Add `debug=true` to reveal `parsedEvent`.

exec: const keyPair    = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveBits']);
console.log('[Crypto] Client public key set:', spkiBase64.substring(0, 24) + '...');
const serverPublicKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', Uint8Array.from(atob(serverPubKeyBase64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', JSON.parse(pm.collectionVariables.get('_ephemeralPrivKeyJwk')), { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const rawSecret       = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPublicKey }, clientPrivKey, 256));
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', rawSecret, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = new Uint8Array(await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info: new TextEncoder().encode('door-event-api-v1') }, hkdfKey, 256));
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] ✅ Decrypted — decode logEvent from Base64 and run through your parser:');
} catch (err) { console.error('[Crypto] ❌', err.message); pm.test('Decryption must succeed', () => { throw err; }); }

request.description:
## GET /doors/events/simulate?logType=DOOR_OPEN — Encrypted

Generates 3 DOOR_OPEN events (0 payload bytes each = 5 bytes total per logEvent) and returns them encrypted.

After decrypting, Base64-decode `logEvent`. You should get exactly 5 bytes: 4 zeroed timestamp bytes + 1 logCode byte with upper nibble = `0` and lower nibble = DOOR_OPEN event type id. There is no payload to parse beyond identifying the event type.

item.name: Permissions
item.description:
CRUD operations for door access permissions.

All routes require a valid `Bearer` token. Responses from `/doors/**` are **ECDH-encrypted** — see the **Permissions — ECDH Encrypted** folder for the encrypted variants with the decryption scripts included.

query.description: Permission type: SMARTPHONE, PASSCODE, or CARD (case-insensitive)

request.description:
## GET /doors/permissions/simulate — Generate synthetic permissions

Generates `count` randomly built, always-valid permission objects of the requested `type`.

Use this to test your client's permission model parsing, date handling, and weekday bitmask interpretation **without creating real database records**.

### Response fields
| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `messageKey` | Sequential index (`"0"`, `"1"`, …) |
| `type` | The requested type (`SMARTPHONE`, `PASSCODE`, `CARD`) |
| `value` | Random, always-valid value for the type (see below) |
| `startDateTime` | Random ISO 8601 UTC — today up to +1 year |
| `endDateTime` | Random ISO 8601 UTC — 1–730 days after `startDateTime` |
| `weekDays` | Random bitmask 1–127. Bit 6 = Monday … bit 0 = Sunday. `127` = all days |

### Value rules by type
- **SMARTPHONE** → random e-mail, e.g. `"swift.user42@example.com"`
- **PASSCODE** → random 4–9 digit string, e.g. `"73851"`
- **CARD** → random non-zero UINT32 string, e.g. `"3187492610"`

### Responses
```json
// 200 OK — SMARTPHONE example
[
  {
    "messageKey": "0",
    "type": "SMARTPHONE",
    "value": "swift.user42@example.com",
    "startDateTime": "2026-05-10T00:00:00Z",
    "endDateTime": "2027-03-14T00:00:00Z",
    "weekDays": 62
  },
  { "messageKey": "1", "type": "SMARTPHONE", "value": "bold.access901@test.io", "startDateTime": "2026-07-22T00:00:00Z", "endDateTime": "2028-01-05T00:00:00Z", "weekDays": 127 }
]

// 400 Bad Request — unknown type
{ "code": "BAD_REQUEST", "description": "Unknown permission type 'FINGERPRINT'. Valid values: [SMARTPHONE, PASSCODE, CARD]" }

// 400 Bad Request — count out of range
{ "code": "BAD_REQUEST", "description": "'count' must be between 1 and 100, got: 0" }
```

## DELETE /doors/{doorId}/permissions/{id}

Permanently removes the permission. Returns `204 No Content` on success.

### Responses
```
204 No Content  (success, no body)
404 Not Found   { "code": "NOT_FOUND", "description": "Permission with id '999' for door '1'" }
```

item.name: Permissions — ECDH Encrypted
item.description:
Encrypted variants of the Permissions endpoints. All `/doors/**` responses are ECDH-encrypted by the server.

Each request uses a pre-request script to generate an ephemeral EC key pair and send the client public key in `X-Client-Public-Key`. The `Tests` script decrypts the response using the shared secret derived via HKDF-SHA256 and AES-256-GCM.

exec: const keyPair = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveKey', 'deriveBits']);
if (!serverPubB64) { console.error('[Crypto] Missing X-Server-Public-Key header'); return; }
const serverPubKey    = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', serverPubBytes, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', privKeyJwk, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const sharedBits      = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPubKey }, clientPrivKey, 256);
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', sharedBits, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info }, hkdfKey, 256);
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));

request.description:
## GET /doors/permissions/simulate — Encrypted

Generates synthetic permissions and returns them AES-256-GCM encrypted.

The `Tests` script:
1. Performs the ECDH key agreement and HKDF-SHA256 derivation
2. Decrypts the response
3. Logs each permission's `type`, `value`, `weekDays`, and `startDateTime` to the console
4. Asserts the array structure and field types

Change `type` to `PASSCODE` or `CARD` to test different value formats.

exec: const keyPair = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveKey', 'deriveBits']);
if (!serverPubB64) { console.error('[Crypto] Missing X-Server-Public-Key header'); return; }
const serverPubKey    = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', serverPubBytes, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', privKeyJwk, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const sharedBits      = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPubKey }, clientPrivKey, 256);
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', sharedBits, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info }, hkdfKey, 256);
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] Decrypted permissions page — totalElements:', page.totalElements);

request.description:
## GET /doors/{doorId}/permissions — Encrypted paginated list

Same as the plain variant but the response body is an ECDH-encrypted envelope:
```json
{ "iv": "<base64>", "ciphertext": "<base64>" }
```
Decrypt with the shared AES-256-GCM key derived from ECDH + HKDF-SHA256 (`info = "door-event-api-v1"`).

The `Tests` script decrypts and logs `totalElements` to the console.

exec: const keyPair = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveKey', 'deriveBits']);
if (!serverPubB64) { console.error('[Crypto] Missing X-Server-Public-Key header'); return; }
const serverPubKey    = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', serverPubBytes, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', privKeyJwk, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const sharedBits      = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPubKey }, clientPrivKey, 256);
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', sharedBits, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info }, hkdfKey, 256);
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] Decrypted full permissions list — count:', permissions.length);

request.description:
## GET /doors/{doorId}/permissions/all — Encrypted full list

Returns the complete permission list in an encrypted envelope. Decrypts to a JSON array.

exec: const keyPair = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveKey', 'deriveBits']);
if (!serverPubB64) { console.error('[Crypto] Missing X-Server-Public-Key header'); return; }
const serverPubKey    = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', serverPubBytes, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', privKeyJwk, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const sharedBits      = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPubKey }, clientPrivKey, 256);
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', sharedBits, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info }, hkdfKey, 256);
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] Decrypted permission — id:', perm.id, 'type:', perm.type, 'weekDays:', perm.weekDays);

request.description:
## GET /doors/{doorId}/permissions/{id} — Encrypted single permission

Decrypts to the `PermissionResponse` object. The `Tests` script logs the permission `id`, `type`, and `weekDays` to the console.

exec: const keyPair = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, true, ['deriveKey', 'deriveBits']);
if (pm.response.code !== 201) { console.log('[Crypto] Non-201 — skipping decryption'); return; }
if (!serverPubB64) { console.error('[Crypto] Missing X-Server-Public-Key header'); return; }
const serverPubKey    = await crypto.subtle.importKey('spki', serverPubBytes, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, []);
const clientPrivKey   = await crypto.subtle.importKey('jwk', privKeyJwk, { name: 'ECDH', namedCurve: 'P-256' }, false, ['deriveBits']);
const sharedBits      = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'ECDH', public: serverPubKey }, clientPrivKey, 256);
const hkdfKey         = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', sharedBits, 'HKDF', false, ['deriveBits']);
const aesKeyBytes     = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits({ name: 'HKDF', hash: 'SHA-256', salt: new Uint8Array(32), info }, hkdfKey, 256);
const aesKey          = await crypto.subtle.importKey('raw', aesKeyBytes, { name: 'AES-GCM' }, false, ['decrypt']);
const { iv: ivB64, ciphertext: ctB64 } = pm.response.json();
const plainBytes      = await crypto.subtle.decrypt({ name: 'AES-GCM', iv: Uint8Array.from(atob(ivB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)) }, aesKey, Uint8Array.from(atob(ctB64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)));
console.log('[Crypto] Created permission — id:', perm.id, 'type:', perm.type);

request.description:
## POST /doors/{doorId}/permissions — Encrypted create

Creates a permission and returns the result in an AES-256-GCM envelope. The `Tests` script decrypts and logs the created permission's ID.
